and said to them , “Sirs, I perceive that the voyage will be with injury and much loss , not only of the cargo and the ship , but also of our lives .”
But the centurion gave more heed to the master and to the owner of the ship than to those things which were spoken by Paul .

Because the haven was not suitable to winter in , the majority advised going to sea from there , if by any means they could reach Phoenix and winter there , which is a port of Crete , looking southwest and northwest .

nama: Njam métar náikái mbormbor waufégh yénmafngoi, yémofem yinjoyénd mer témorwén álighét. Féyo táf yurtoyénd werégh téfén ndimbal boutoene a nuwanoyénd Krit arakaf téndomé. 14 Wénde ndimbal mbormbor yétkwén Yambérmawér Mbormboram eutfoi fá a yénmaufetai kétamé férta. 15 Mbormbor témaufetau minde muyafé ndimbal boutfaf. Féyotarofnar tamorwén wawerégh téfnáram sái taweretawét ndimbal bout mbarkánde kétán mbormborfaf. Féyo yumyoyénd ndimbal bout mbormboram yéngwanai. 16 Yénd yénamé yéngmormén Kauda fér néndkéfamé. Bout wawerégh téfnáram wénmauyoyénd Boutoene ndingi nutambén ménát wanjarghét. 17 Féyo yémofem yarmboyénd ndingi ndimbal bout tukét. Yéné soramé yémofem wumbégh yafngoyénd ángéghe ndimbal bout ménát taikrot. Ménamén fá férfér tamorwén nde mété táf kásmetawét wíman Siritisén, yémofem yeworyénd boutoene wérégh tot téfén yumyoyénd ndimbal bout yéngwanayéng mbormboram. 18 Ndimbal mbormbor mindefifi taufetau, féyo ámb efghon ámb rokar taranégh ewafngoyénd ndimbal boutta. 19 Nambiomén efghon yémofem taranégh ewafngoyénd ámb bout ásáfoghmén rokar. 20 Tárfár efghon yéndfem yau tafandawém efogh a kénd. Ndimbal mbormbor mé ténmaufetau muyafé a yénd féyo yénmormén a téfene kamnang nefoyénd awanjarghét.
english: When the south wind blew softly , supposing that they had obtained their purpose , they weighed anchor and sailed along Crete , close to shore.
But before long , a stormy wind beat down from shore, which is called Euroclydon .
When the ship was caught and couldn’ t face the wind, we gave way to it and were driven along .
Running under the lee of a small island called Clauda , we were able , with difficulty , to secure the boat .
After they had hoisted it up , they used cables to help reinforce the ship . Fearing that they would run aground on the Syrtis sand bars, they lowered the sea anchor , and so were driven along .
As we labored exceedingly with the storm, the next day they began to throw things overboard.
On the third day , they threw out the ship ’s tackle with their own hands.
When neither sun nor stars shone on us for many days , and no small storm pressed on us , all hope that we would be saved was now taken away .

nama: Yéné téfnan yéndfem yau njamke tánetawém ndené teke. Fol nokayoi emndai ár: "Fém fai káyáretawét tafaf njam tamorwén kété Krit féran, fémofem yau yéné wasérmbogh so tinjongayénd a té kawaghnat. 22 Wénde sénonjo yéndon ewamnendan fém, yau férfér em ménamén emo ámb yau ghérsé so yétarne, mé ndimbal boutro so nawasérmbote. 23 Yénd wém Ngánjánene, a yéndon yáwefnotan yáyot. Sémbáran yémon ténmáfárete njafar si résagh ár 24 ta waufroi, 'Fol, yau férfér ném! Fém so némakai ufoghan Rom tuk warsagh ároene rére. Ménamén fenemén Ngánjánam so tawanjarngé efe méinyotio em ndimbal boutan.' 25 Féyo férfér yau em! Yénd mat wém ndené Ngánjánam so yafrote mbarkánd némé fá norayai. 26 Wéndende, ndimbal bout so nirone ámb féran." Ndimbal bout niroyéng
english: When they had been long without food, Paul stood up in the middle of them and said , “Sirs, you should have listened to me , and not have set sail from Crete and have gotten this injury and loss .
Now I exhort you to cheer up , for there will be no loss of life among you , but only of the ship .
For there stood by me this night an angel, belonging to the God whose I am and whom I serve ,
saying , ‘Don’ t be afraid , Paul . You must stand before Caesar . Behold , God has granted you all those who sail with you .’
Therefore , sirs, cheer up! For I believe God , that it will be just as it has been spoken to me .
But we must run aground on a certain island .”

nama: 14 sémbáran yénd ténmimndau mbormboram yénamé Mediterian arakafamé. Wénde sémbár néndkéf ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár mat náyátoyénd yénd kaka náyátoyém mbandfaf. 28 Yémofem yufotaménd nuoene kénjkam ndené 40 mité. Yéné soramé wéi yufotaménd a yinyoyénd 30 mité kénjkam témorwén. 29 Ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár férfér tamorwén ménamén mété táf kásmetawét wíman. Féyo yémofem ewitaménd 4 werégh tot téfén ndimbal boutoene kwémb tambén a fá Ngánjánafé orayégh nufngoyénd efogh kwayaghat.
english: But when the fourteenth night had come , as we were driven back and forth in the Adriatic Sea, about midnight the sailors surmised that they were drawing near to some land .
They took soundings and found twenty fathoms . After a little while , they took soundings again , and found fifteen fathoms .
Fearing that we would run aground on rocky ground, they let go four anchors from the stern , and wished for daylight .

nama: Bout ásáfogh ár ménde tamorwén sái téfrangongayénd ndimbal bout. Féyo yémofem yeworyénd ndingi arakafot. Fá yéta kwankwane álighét nufngoyénd. Fá norayayénd, "Yénd yéngém kétán ndimbal bout sénkofaf ménát ndimbal boutoene werégh tot téfén so taweworngém arakafot." Wénde kuindawét. 31 Wénde Folém emndai fiyaré sénko ár a fiyaré, "Ndimbal bout ásáfogh ár yau njam emor ndimbal boutan, féfene kamnang ámb yau yém ménát awanjarghét." 32 Féyo fiyaram yénfayénd ángégh árké teretawét ndingi a nuyai arakafot.
english: As the sailors were trying to flee out of the ship and had lowered the boat into the sea , pretending that they would lay out anchors from the bow ,
Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers , “ Unless these stay in the ship , you can ’ t be saved .”
Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the boat and let it fall off .

nama: Fauréghétro témorwén Folém ewamnendai njamke nénet, a emndai, "14 efogh fém té tam mámé mwighé a ámb njamke yau tánetati. 34 Yéndon ewamnendan fém, féyo, njamke tánetangi, ghérsé sái mé em. Yéné yafrotati a fém so mer em."
english: While the day was coming on , Paul begged them all to take some food , saying , “ Today is the fourteenth day that you wait and continue fasting, having taken nothing .
Therefore I beg you to take some food , for this is for your safety; for not a hair will perish from any of your heads .”

nama: Si soramé, Folém yanayéng ámb féskaf flawé karéf a kánjo si yaramayéng Ngánjáne. Féyo méinyotio árfene sáfrére, yémo yétfoi féskaf flawé a ténetau. 36 Fá méinyotio náuwétaménd a ámb njamke enetaménd. 37 Kété tamorwén méinyotio 276 ár ndimbal boutan. 38 Njam yémofem enetaménd a eremngoi, yémofem tawitawét flawé baik kétán arakafot ndimbal bout ofat.
english: When he had said this and had taken bread, he gave thanks to God in the presence of all ; then he broke it and began to eat .
Then they all cheered up , and they also took food .
In all , we were two hundred seventy-six souls on the ship .
When they had eaten enough , they lightened the ship , throwing out the wheat into the sea .

nama: Ámb akwan, yémofem yau yáyámngoyénd mband, wénde yémofem yinjoyénd trewar ménáyafé miri mbandafé. Féyo yémofem mwighé yinjoyénd ménát wérset miriamé. 40 Yémofem enfayénd werégh téfén ángégh féyo engéfrangoyénd arakafan. Táfandio yémofem eferyénd ángégh árkéménayam teretawét ndimbal bout waknyéghmén. Yénamayo yémofem yakayoyénd sáimai ndimbal bout sénko tambén a mbormboram ndimbal bout résagh yafngoi kétán mirifaf. 41 Wénde ndimbal bout miri mbandot náyátoi. Féyo ndimbal bout sénko nékmboi kété yau kimndau, a ndimbal sófam yésmai ndimbal bout kwémbta méinyotio yiroryéng.
english: When it was day , they didn’ t recognize the land , but they noticed a certain bay with a beach, and they decided to try to drive the ship onto it .
Casting off the anchors, they left them in the sea , at the same time untying the rudder ropes . Hoisting up the foresail to the wind , they made for the beach.
But coming to a place where two seas met , they ran the vessel aground . The bow struck and remained immovable, but the stern began to break up by the violence of the waves.

nama: Fiyaré méndefnar tamorwén efe yau so kakmetawét tunat a kéngongayénd, féyo yémofem mwighé téfrendawét kwéfkwéf méngo árfe ngiot. 43 Wéndende, fiyaré sénko ár ménde témorwén Fole wanjarghét a yémo yau taumyotau yéné yam wafroghét. Wéndende, "Méinyotio ár efe mat em akmégh, katnangi arakafot, a kakmetangi tunat. 44 Ámb árém táf ewaitotat. Nawéretat wén karfan o ndimbal bout karéfkaréfan." Féyo fá nufaryénd merro kétán tunat.
english: The soldiers ’ counsel was to kill the prisoners , so that none of them would swim out and escape .
But the centurion , desiring to save Paul , stopped them from their purpose , and commanded that those who could swim should throw themselves overboard first to go toward the land ;
and the rest should follow , some on planks and some on other things from the ship . So they all escaped safely to the land .

nama: Njam yénd merro nufaryém, yéndfem yáyamngoyém fér yétkwén Malta. 2 Fér ár efe tákmangérwén yémofem yénmumyoyénd mere a fútar náyátoyém. Yémofem fés yétayaménd a yénmumyoyénd yénd méinyotio ménamén nu kafamndau a kurférmén.
english: When we had escaped , then they learned that the island was called Malta .
The natives showed us uncommon kindness ; for they kindled a fire and received us all , because of the present rain and because of the cold .

nama: Folém ámb fés sut enwanai a efayoi fését. Fés waftayam yéméindai kamban étorghét a yararyéng yáne ngángé. 4 Njam fér árém yinjoyénd kamban térnyangérwén yáne ngángayan, fá korayangi yéfe kénjúnro, "Yéné ár mété yém ár ngi téfnár! Fá yau kolitote arakafan, wénde ngánjánam yau yumyo ghérsat." 5 Wénde Folém yéfnendai kamban fését yéneghoi a ámb némé kérté yau yanai. 6 Ár kemnawét yáfaf ndené mbwe kar so tarenau o sune kér témorwén. Wénde teke awanjengégh soramé yau némndafi nufrotam yáfaf, yéfefaf mwighé nufaryénd a norayayénd, "Fá siyém ngánján."
english: But when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks and laid them on the fire , a viper came out because of the heat and fastened on his hand .

When the natives saw the creature hanging from his hand , they said to one another , “ No doubt this man is a murderer , whom , though he has escaped from the sea , yet Justice has not allowed to live .”
However he shook off the creature into the fire , and wasn’ t harmed.

But they expected that he would have swollen or fallen down dead suddenly, but when they watched for a long time and saw nothing bad happen to him , they changed their minds and said that he was a god .

nama: Gafana témorwén Fablis yéné féran, yáne ámb mband kété témorwén. Fablis témorwén ngárám ár téfefaf a yénmumyoi yánjo méngot nambio efogh ámnjoghét. 8 Yárefé enjne tékmangérwén yameyan fifi waftamén a oghanemén. Fol nénglawai kénjút Ngánjánafé orayéghét a ngángé yéfayoi yáfaf a mer yiyátoi. 9 Njam yéné yam nufrotam, yéné férta enjne téfnár tanmorwén a mer táyátotau. 10 Yémofem ténmutémndawét yénd tárfár ende, a yénd njam álighétro ténmorwén, yémofem ténmarametawét téfene méndefé rokar. Fol nufaryéng Rom
english: Now in the neighborhood of that place were lands belonging to the chief man of the island , named Publius , who received us and courteously entertained us for three days .
The father of Publius lay sick of fever and dysentery . Paul entered in to him , prayed , and laying his hands on him , healed him .
Then when this was done , the rest also who had diseases in the island came and were cured .
They also honored us with many honors ; and when we sailed , they put on board the things that we needed .

nama: Nambio ghérare soramé mbormboram yiroyéng ndimbal bout. Ndimbal bout yéméghangérmén Maltan mbormbormén yunjfan. Yéné témorwén Aleksandriamén ndimbal bout a kété tárwén sómbio wafroghmén ngánján ndimbal bout sénkoyan. Yénd néngufaryém ndimbal boute. 12 Yénd ngérsawém arakafamé nungufaryém Sirakus tesen a yénmorangérmén kété nambio efogh. 13 Kétándmé yénd nufaryém Regium tesen. Ámb efghon náikái mbormbor waufégh yafngoi, sómbio efogh soramé yénd nufaryém Fotioli tesen. 14 Kété yéndfem ewinjoyém Yesuene waitogh ár, emofem yénmumyoyénd yémafé ámnjoghét 7 efghot. A féyo yénd néngwanoyém Rom. 15 Ámb Yesuene waitogh ár Rommén náyárayénd téfenemén a enmormén téfe wanféghét Afius térarégh sifayat a méngotu yétkwén Nambio wan téfnár méngot. Njam Folém ewinjoi fá, yémon kánjo yaramai Ngánjáne a frengégh yanai. 16 Yénd njam nufaryém Rom, Fol yumyoyénd yambia ámnjoghét fiyaram tambetau. |CN02055B.TIF|col||© 1996 David C Cook. Used by permission.|Folém efangoi efalo yátogh ár Rommén (Acts 28.16)|28.16 Folém táitotau mer si Romén wambégh mbanmbanan
english: After three months , we set sail in a ship of Alexandria which had wintered in the island , whose figurehead was “ The Twin Brothers .”
Touching at Syracuse , we stayed there three days .
From there we circled around and arrived at Rhegium . After one day , a south wind sprang up , and on the second day we came to Puteoli ,
where we found brothers, and were entreated to stay with them for seven days . So we came to Rome .
From there the brothers, when they heard of us , came to meet us as far as The Market of Appius and The Three Taverns . When Paul saw them , he thanked God and took courage .
When we entered into Rome , the centurion delivered the prisoners to the captain of the guard , but Paul was allowed to stay by himself with the soldier who guarded him .

nama: Nambio efogh soramé yémo eumendai Ju árfene sénko ár a Fol norayai yéfefaf, "Tane fútaréf, yéndon némé yau tafrotan téfene árfefaf o téfene ákifene túmén yam, wénde yénd werayénd Jerusalemén a wéramayénd Rom árfene ngángat. 18 Yémofem kwémotawét yénd a ménde tamorwén ta utorghét, ménamén yaufi yau yinjoyénd tafaf árkémamén kér so kwémorwén. 19 Ju sénko ár korayawét tafaf, féyo yéné yamam kwéméindangé yénd sái korayangén Kaisar náyáreta tane si, ndemamén tane ámb yau témorwén wánangogh tane árfefaf. 20 Féyo yéndo té kénmarawetan fém yénan álighét ménát yéndo so némaufrotan némamén yénd wém kwéfkwéf méngon. Yéné ménamén yém yéndo efalo yiyátotan Keriso, efe yém Israil árfene kamnang té yénmormén."
english: After three days Paul called together those who were the leaders of the Jews . When they had come together , he said to them , “ I , brothers, though I had done nothing against the people or the customs of our fathers , still was delivered prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans ,
who , when they had examined me , desired to set me free , because there was no cause of death in me .
But when the Jews spoke against it , I was constrained to appeal to Caesar , not that I had anything about which to accuse my nation .
For this cause therefore I asked to see you and to speak with you . For because of the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.”

nama: Yémofem yawangoyénd, "Yéndfem ámb yau némé férén ewinjoyém Judiamén fenemén. Ámb yau téfene ár efe enmormén kétándmé nafroyénd o norayayénd yaufi si fenemén. 22 Wénde yénd ménde yéném áyárghét fene si némé em, ménamén yénd mat yéném árém norayat yaufi si marataye yéné totér mérénmén."
english: They said to him , “ We neither received letters from Judea concerning you , nor did any of the brothers come here and report or speak any evil of you .
But we desire to hear from you what you think . For , as concerning this sect, it is known to us that everywhere it is spoken against .”

nama: Yémofem yufoyénd ámb efogh Fole álighét. Njam yéné efogh nawerai, tárfár ár enmormén mérne yéné sifayat ánde fá tékmangérwén. Yémo táitotau yéfefaf akw kétán férégh, tawawafenau Ngánján limánéghmén, a kétamé Mosesene nák a kétamé Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfenemén. Yémo tamndetau ménát yaufiyamta awengoghét a ménát awengoghét Yesuefaf. 24 Ámb árém efalo tiyátotawét némé fá korayau, wénde ámb árém yau efalo tiyátotawét. 25 Fá kéwitawét yénémamén yéfenjo kénjún. Njam yémofem frangoghét yafngoyénd, Fol norayai, "Ngánján Mbérmbéram norayai efalo si Ngánjánene mat yátogh ár Aisaya kénjúmé féfene ákifefaf:
english: When they had appointed him a day , many people came to him at his lodging . He explained to them , testifying about God ’s Kingdom, and persuading them concerning Jesus , both from the law of Moses and from the prophets , from morning until evening .
Some believed the things which were spoken , and some disbelieved.
When they didn’ t agree among themselves , they departed after Paul had spoken one message : “ The Holy Spirit spoke rightly through Isaiah the prophet to our fathers ,

nama: "'Kuwano yéné árfefaf a koraya, "Fém so náyáretati wénde mat yau so náyátotati. Fémofem so yéfandati wénde yau so tayamngongi.
english: saying , ‘ Go to this people and say , in hearing, you will hear, but will in no way understand . In seeing , you will see , but will in no way perceive .

nama: Ménamén yéné árfene tikéf té nénamndai táikér, yémofem táikér yéfandat áyárghét, a yémofem yéfenjo ghékéf té eumbértongi. Féyo mété táf yau néretat yéfenjo ghékfe, a yau náyáretat yéfenjo téngane, a efalo yau yiyátotat yéfenjo tikfe a kawengongi ménát yéndon so mer táyáton fá."'"
english: For this people ’s heart has grown callous. Their ears are dull of hearing . Their eyes they have closed . Lest they should see with their eyes , hear with their ears , understand with their heart , and would turn again , then I would heal them .’ + 28:27 Isaiah 6:9-10

nama: Yénémaménnde yénd Fol ménde wém fém mat so em Ngánjánene wanjarégh té yéngramai kétán Téndo árfefaf, a fá so náyáretat! 29 {c{ ...f* {c{28.29 Yau siménj
english: “ Be it known therefore to you that the salvation of God is sent to the nations , and they will listen.”
When he had said these words, the Jews departed, having a great dispute among themselves.

nama: Sómbio yunjéf Fol yémorangérmén kété yáne mani faimén méngon a taumyotau fá efe tanmormén yá fandaghét. 31 Yémo tawaufrotau Ngánján limánéghmén a tawatametau Yuré Yesu Kerisoenemén férférofnar a emo yau tékmbongai.
english: Paul stayed two whole years in his own rented house and received all who were coming to him ,
preaching God ’ s Kingdom and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness , without hindrance.

nama: Fol, Yesu Kerisoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, nénamndai Yesuene muyafé ár a fénataye náyátoi Ngánjánene mer si waitoghét.
english: Paul , a servant of Jesus Christ , called to be an apostle, set apart for the Good News of God ,

nama: Ngánjánam unyéghofnar si yiyátoi yéné mer si tándái, soramé nufrotam. Ngánjánene mat yátogh árém yéfarotawét yénémamén Bukan. 3 Yéné mer si siyém yánjo Ambumoenemén, fá náráftam Deifidene mérnan fifie. 4 A njam nanoi kérta ménaye Ngánján Mbérmbér waftaye, fá té yuyafoi sái témorwén Ngánjánene Ambum Yesu Keriso téfene Yuré. 5 Yá kénjúmé Ngánjánam yénmaramai ngárám Kerisoene yétkwén téfnan yáne muyafé ár, ménát sái elimndam Téndo ár áyárghét árké yéném kétamé ndimbal mwighata. 6 Fém wéi fété em yéfefaf, efe té náyátoyénd Yesu Kerisoene.
english: which he promised before through his prophets in the holy Scriptures ,
concerning his Son , who was born of the offspring of David according to the flesh ,
who was declared to be the Son of God with power according to the Spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead , Jesus Christ our Lord ,
through whom we received grace and apostleship for obedience of faith among all the nations for his name ’s sake ;
among whom you are also called to belong to Jesus Christ ;

nama: A yéndon yéfarotan méinyotio féfeyot kétán efe em Romén efe euwete Ngánjánam a namndetat yáne njúnnjúnofnar ár. Ngárám a krufrát féfeyot Ngánjáneta téfene Afé a kétamé Yuré Yesu Keriso. Folene ménde yafoghét Romét
english: to all who are in Rome , beloved of God , called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ .

nama: Fronde, yéndon kánjo ewarametan tanjo Ngánjáne Yesu Keriso kénjúmé féfeyot ményotio, ménamén féfene ndimbal mwighé téfnan mband marat afrogh té néngufngo. 9 Ngánjáne yéndon yausáfotan tane tikfe ménáyan yáne Ambumoene si waitoghan, fá siyém tane ghékéf fayogh ár ndernáye ekmboghofnar yéndon ewafarotan fém 10 tane Ngánjánafé orayégh kénjún ményotio efghon. A yénd Ngánjánafé noraiwam ndené sénonjo soramé Ngánjánene méndeyan end so kasawérngé tayot sái wéném féfefaf. 11 Yénd minde ménde wém féfe fandaghét, ménát yéndon so eférmetan féfefaf ámb mbérmbére tambén ménát so táyátongé muyafé. 12 Ndené fété fém a yénd sái nawáutánam mere téfene ndimbal mwighaye.
english: First , I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you , that your faith is proclaimed throughout the whole world .
For God is my witness , whom I serve in my spirit in the Good News of his Son , how unceasingly I make mention of you always in my prayers,
requesting, if by any means now at last I may be prospered by the will of God to come to you .
For I long to see you , that I may impart to you some spiritual gift , to the end that you may be established ;
that is , that I with you may be encouraged in you , each of us by the other ’s faith , both yours and mine .

nama: Tane fútar, yénd ménde wém fém sái mat em ndené yénd ménde yéta kwém tárfár mbinotma sái kwénmorwén féfefaf, wénde yénanotio sénonjo, ámb yamam wékmbote. Yénd ménde wém yéndo sái násáfotan féfe kénjún a so yéfandan mer mbwe, ndené fété yéndon kés yéfandan ámb Téndo ár kénjún. 14 Yéndo táf so ewaufrotan yáne mer si méinyotio árét. Yéndo so ewafrotan yáne mer si árét, Grikmén árét o yau Grikmén árét, a endefaf em káfeté, o endefaf em káfetafnar. 15 Yéné téfnan yénd ménde wém mer si waitoghét wéi féfefaf efe em Romén. Mer sioene wafté
english: Now I don’ t desire to have you unaware , brothers, that I often planned to come to you ( and was hindered so far ), that I might have some fruit among you also , even as among the rest of the Gentiles .
I am debtor both to Greeks and to foreigners, both to the wise and to the foolish .
So as much as is in me , I am eager to preach the Good News to you also who are in Rome .

nama: Yénd yau wáiwái wém yéné mer simén, ménamén yéné siyém Ngánjánene wafté wanjarghét méinyotio árét emofem efalo yiyátotat, fronde yéfeyot Israil árfet a yénamayo Téndo árét. 17 Ménamén yéné mer sian mbarkánd yam kétamé yéném Ngánjáneta nénufane, a mbarkánd yam árké yém ménaye yém ndimbal mwighaye kétamé frondeta kétán soramé,{a{ ...f* {a{1.17 Ménamén yéné mer sian mbarkánd yam kétamé yéném Ngánjáneta nénufane, a mbarkánd yam árké yém ménaye yém ndimbal mwighaye kétamé frondeta kétán soramé, This Good News tells us how God makes us right in his sight. This is accomplished from start to finish by faith.(NLT)-translate it again ndené fété kés yéfarotaménd: "Mbarkánd ár so emor ndimbal mwighaye." Ngánjánene nékw méinyotio árfefaf
english: For I am not ashamed of the Good News of Christ, because it is the power of God for salvation for everyone who believes , for the Jew first , and also for the Greek .
For in it is revealed God ’s righteousness from faith to faith . As it is written , “ But the righteous shall live by faith .”

nama: Ngánjánene nékw nufrote njafarta yéfefaf méinyotio ngánjánofnar ghérsafé ár a yaufiyam wafrogh téfnár emofem yengwitrotat efalo yéfene kénjúta. 19 Fá so fété mat em Ngánján efe yém, ménamén Ngánjánamyo té ewauyafoi sáfotro yéfeyot. 20 Kétamé mband marat nufrotam, árém té yinjoyénd mband a njafar. Yénayan méinyotio rokar Ngánjánam ewafrotam, yémofem féyotaro so efandat safénro yáne fandaghofnar yam, yáne akegh wafté a ménde. Féyo kété ámb yau endeyot si yém sái noraya, yénd yau mat ténmorwén Ngánjánenemén efaloyam.
english: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men who suppress the truth in unrighteousness,
because that which is known of God is revealed in them , for God revealed it to them .

For the invisible things of him since the creation of the world are clearly seen , being perceived through the things that are made , even his everlasting power and divinity, that they may be without excuse.

nama: Ndemamén fá mat em Ngánjánenemén, wénde yémofem yau kor táwefnotawét ndené fété Ngánján, o yau kor tarametawét kánjo si yáfaf. Wénde fá yau kor kemnawét safénro. Yéfene mwighé nénamndayénd kwéfitayan a orsaghan. 22 Ndemamén fá namndetat káfeté ár em, wénde fá em mwighémwighafnar; 23 Yémofem yékenat sái yáwefnotat ndimbal Ngánjáne efe yau so kér yém. Wénde, yémofem ewafrotat unyégh ngánján yéfendio. Yémofem ewáwefnotat mewangat árké em árnit efe kér so em. Yémofem wéi ewafrotat árké nérétat mennit, wérárnit a kambannit, a yémofem ewáwefnotat.
english: Because knowing God , they didn’ t glorify him as God , and didn’ t give thanks , but became vain in their reasoning , and their senseless heart was darkened .
Professing themselves to be wise , they became fools ,
and traded the glory of the incorruptible God for the likeness of an image of corruptible man , and of birds , four-footed animals , and creeping things .

nama: Féyo Ngánjánam eumyoi yaufiyam méndeyotro tikfan kétán máméghéraye walot ménde wifogh yéngwafngoyénd yéfene fifiyan fárofáro. 25 Fá nákenayénd efalo yátoghét Ngánjánenemén.efaloyam. Wénde yémofem yanayénd unyégh. Yémofem táwáwefnotawét a kásáfotawét rokarat Ngánjánam árké ewafrotam. Wénde yémofem nakenayénd Ngánján wáwefnoghét. Fá yéné yém emo ewafrotam yéné méinyotio rokar. Méinyotio árém táf yuwetat fá tuot! Efalo. Yéné siyém efalofifi.
english: Therefore God also gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to uncleanness, that their bodies should be dishonored among themselves ;
who exchanged the truth of God for a lie , and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator , who is blessed forever . Amen.

nama: Yéné téfnan, Ngánjánam eramai kétán wáiwáiafé ménde yam wafroghét. Yéfene amafam yengoyénd árfé kémégh yam kétán amafro kémégh yamfaf. 27 Ndené yameyo árém yéfrangoyénd amafafé kémégh yam a nufngoyénd ár ghakér ro yam fárofáro. Ár wáiwáiafé yam nefngoyénd fárofáro, a yanayénd fai yéfenjo yaufimén.
english: For this reason , God gave them up to vile passions . For their women changed the natural function into that which is against nature .

Likewise also the men , leaving the natural function of the woman , burned in their lust toward one another , men doing what is inappropriate with men , and receiving in themselves the due penalty of their error .

nama: Táf yéné ár nakenayénd sái tafarotawét Ngánján, féyo Ngánjánam eumyoi yéfene mwighé ewasérmbotam. Féyo yéné téfnan, yémofem tawafrotawét yaufi yam árkéméné yau so tawafrotawét. 29 Fá té ewafoi méinyotio fénaté áyáréghofnar yamam, yaufi, nité a mámáfi. Fá sem waramnjaghro, ngi, ásmégh, wasogh a ár fayogh. Fá sem si tiyárégh, 30 a unyégh si wafrogh árfenemén, Ngánján méndménd, utmanéghofnar, ndimbalndimbal mwighé, a awarmbogh mwighé. Yémofem nemnat tárfár ende yaufi wafroghét, a yémofem yau eyáretat yéferafaf. 31 Fá yau mat em némé yém mbarkánd. Yémofem eráfnat yéfene unyéghofnar si. Yémofem yau euwetat ámb ár a yau em ghérghérsisé ámb árfefaf. 32 Fá mat em Ngánjánene mbarkánd yam yénat emofem ewafrotat yéné yam so em kér. Wénde yémofem ménjtaye mé ewafrotat yéné yaufi yam. Minde mámáfiot, yémofem ewawanjat ámb ár ménát sái ewafrotat wéi.
english: Even as they refused to have God in their knowledge , God gave them up to a reprobate mind , to do those things which are not fitting;
being filled with all unrighteousness, sexual immorality, wickedness , covetousness , malice ; full of envy , murder , strife , deceit , evil habits, secret slanderers,
backbiters , hateful to God , insolent , arrogant , boastful, inventors of evil things , disobedient to parents ,
without understanding, covenant breakers, without natural affection, unforgiving, unmerciful;
who , knowing the ordinance of God , that those who practice such things are worthy of death , not only do the same , but also approve of those who practice them .

nama: Yénémamén, fém mé nemne fém sái moghan ekmeta ndené ár, wénde fém ndené mámáfiyo ném, a féfeyot ámb yau so ném uwangogh! Njam fém noraye fá em yaufi a so tékratan em, fém fémyo moghan nakmeta, ménamén fémo emofem njam moghan ekmeta ámb ár ewafrote yéné yamyo wéi. 2 Yénd mat yéném ndené Ngánján siyém mbarkánd yém njam yémo moghan ekmeta fá emofem ewafrotat yéné yam. 3 Fémo yéférmete ámogh yéfefaf a ewafrote wéi yéné yamyo. Fém nemne so kátnongi Ngánjánene moghta? 4 Nawafarotati ndené Ngánján siyém ramégh kér ro a métar téfefaf. Fá nawanjete teke soramé yémo so tékratan yénékmete yénd. Fém té nemne yéné siyém fifiofnar fefaf? Fém fiya mat kamndé ndené Ngánjánam ewaramete fe efoghféneféne awengoghét. Fá nawanjete métare, ménát fém so kawengo yaufiyamta.
english: Therefore you are without excuse, O man , whoever you are who judge . For in that which you judge another , you condemn yourself . For you who judge practice the same things .
We know that the judgment of God is according to truth against those who practice such things .
Do you think this , O man who judges those who practice such things , and do the same , that you will escape the judgment of God ?
Or do you despise the riches of his goodness , forbearance, and patience , not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance ?

nama: Wénde fém nakena awengoghét yéné ende fém némor. Fém yau ghérghérsisé ném ménamén yaufi yammén ndené fémofem tawafrote. Yéné téfnan, fémo téméinda Ngánján nékwét féfendio. Féyo, yémo so tékratan tékmangé fém minde yéné efghon njam yémo moghan tákmangé méinyotio ár. Yéné efghon, yémo so yuyafote ndernáye nékw so yém yéné árfefaf emofem ewafrotat yaufiyam. Méinyotio ár mat so em ndené yáne mogh árét siyém mbarkánd. 6 Ngánjánam so ewaramete méinyotio fénatayero fai ároe yénayan yémofem némé tawafrotat. 7 Yéfeyot emofem ménjtaye yéngwafrotat mer, yéronjat ndimbal yétkwén, utmanghét a kérofnar ghérsat ndené Ngánjánam so eramete, Ngánjánam so tawaramngé tuot ghérsé. 8 Wénde yéfeyot efe fifi ménde wifarghan tém a emofem tékarotat efalo si a táitotat yaufi, kété so yém minde nékw. 9 Kété so yém kértayam a tékrát méinyotio árét emofem ewafrotat yaufi: fronde so yém Ju árfefaf, yénamayo Téndo árfefaf. 10 Wénde ndimbal yétkwén, utmanégh a krufrát yém yéfeyot méinyotio árét emofem tawafrotat mer: fronde Ju árfeyot, yénamayo Téndo árfeyot. 11 Ménamén Ngánjánam moghan ekmete méinyotio ár ámbrérero.
english: But according to your hardness and unrepentant heart you are treasuring up for yourself wrath in the day of wrath , revelation, and of the righteous judgment of God ,
who “ will pay back to everyone according to their works :”
to those who by perseverance in well-doing seek for glory , honor , and incorruptibility, eternal life ;
but to those who are self-seeking and don’ t obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, will be wrath , indignation ,
oppression, and anguish on every soul of man who does evil , to the Jew first , and also to the Greek .
But glory , honor , and peace go to every man who does good , to the Jew first , and also to the Greek .
For there is no partiality with God .

nama: Ár emofem yaufiyam tawafrotat nákofnaye wéi so kér em nákofnaye, a ár emofem yaufiyam tawafrotat nákanyo so moghan em náke. 13 Ménamén yau fá mbarkánd em efe áyáréghro náyáretat nákfaf Ngánjánrére, wénde fá emofem ewaitotat nák fá sof tiyátongé mbarkánd Yáne sáfrére. 14 Téndo árfefaf yau em Ngánjánene nák. Wénde yémofem mé té ewafrotat mbarkánd yam ndené yéné nák ndernáye norayat. Yémofem ewafrotat mbarkánd yam ménamén fá ndernáye nemnat yém mbarkánd. Yéné ende fá nauyafotat ndené yéfefaf em yéné nák yéfene mwighayan, ndemamén fá mafina em Mosesene nák. 15 Yémofem ndené yufarotat Ngánjánene nák nafarotaménd yéfene tikfan. Yéfene emargham tawaufrongé ánde yém mer a yaufi. Ámb efghon yéfene mwighé noraye: "Fémo té tafrote ámb yaufi.' A ámb efghon yéfene mwighé noraye: "Fémo tafrote némé mbarkánd yém.' 16 Yéné fiyém si ndené yéndo ewaufrotan árét. Ngánján mat yém méinyotio árké ár kwankwane néngemnat. Ámb efghon Ngánjánam so moghan tákmangé méinyotio ár Yesu Keriso kénjúmé. Ju ár a nák
english: For as many as have sinned without the law will also perish without the law . As many as have sinned under the law will be judged by the law .
For it isn’ t the hearers of the law who are righteous before God , but the doers of the law will be justified
( for when Gentiles who don’ t have the law do by nature the things of the law , these , not having the law , are a law to themselves ,
in that they show the work of the law written in their hearts , their conscience testifying with them , and their thoughts among themselves accusing or else excusing them )
in the day when God will judge the secrets of men , according to my Good News , by Jesus Christ .

nama: Fém njam namndeta Ju ár ném, fém njam námyota kétán Ngánjánene nákfaf a nawarsa fene fénaté fútaryammén Ngánjánafé. 18 Néméwét, fém mat ném némé ménde Ngánján yém. Fém mat ném ndené mbárkándyam siyém mer em ménamén fém té ewatametaménd yáne nák. 19 Fém njam féyotaro ném ndené fém wambégh ném yéfeyot kwéfkwéf árét, a kwayaghé yéfeyot efe em kwéfitayan, 20 watamegh ár onon árfene, a watamegh ár ambumtértérafene, ménamén fém féyotaro ném ndené Ngánjánene nákam ewaramete féyotio mat a efalo. 21 Mer fété, njam fémo ewatamete ámb ár, némamén wét fémyo yau nawatameta? Fémo emndeta ámb ár oro yau, wénde fém oro té nufrote? 22 Fém efe noraye ndené amafanyo awanégh yau, wénde fémo amafanyo ewaneta? Fém noraye fémo méndméndat efanda mewang, wénde fémo engérsa rokar kétándmé mewangoene wawefnogh méngota? 23 Fém efe nawarsa ménamén nák mat ném, wénde yau yutémndati Ngánján ménaye eráfnati nák. 24 Bukam norayai yénémamén: 'Ménamén fém Israil ár, Téndo ár norayat yaufi si Ngánjánefaf.'
english: Indeed you bear the name of a Jew , rest on the law , glory in God ,
know his will , and approve the things that are excellent , being instructed out of the law ,
and are confident that you yourself are a guide of the blind , a light to those who are in darkness ,
a corrector of the foolish , a teacher of babies, having in the law the form of knowledge and of the truth.
You therefore who teach another , don’ t you teach yourself ? You who preach that a man shouldn’ t steal , do you steal ?
You who say a man shouldn’ t commit adultery , do you commit adultery ? You who abhor idols , do you rob temples ?
You who glory in the law , do you dishonor God by disobeying the law ?
For “ the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you ,” just as it is written .

nama: Ghéngan néfégh yam tukényo yém táf fémo njam ewaitota nák, wénde fémo njam eráfna nák, fém ndenanit nénamnde fém yau ghéngan néfégh yaman némormén. 26 Ár efe njam ghéngan néfghofnar yém sái ewaitota nák, fá so tiyátoi ndené ghéngan néfégh yaman yémormén. 27 Efaloye, fá efe yau ghéngan yénfayénd fifie a mé ewaitota nák yémo so moghan ekmete fém mé nák rafarégh ár ném, ndemamén fene faroghmén nák yém a ghéngan néfégh yam. 28 Ár efe Ju ár ghérsé yémor fifiyero yau yém efalo Ju ár. A ghéngan néfégh yau fifiménro yam yém. 29 Yau, efalo Ju ár siyém endene tikéf yém mbarkánd Ngánjánafé a ghéngan néfghét yam siyém ghéngan néfghét yam tikfan, Ngánján Mbérmbére, yau faroghmén náke. Yéné sáfot árfene áuwégh yau yéném árta, wénde kétamé Ngánjánta.
english: For circumcision indeed profits , if you are a doer of the law , but if you are a transgressor of the law , your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
If therefore the uncircumcised keep the ordinances of the law , won’ t his uncircumcision be accounted as circumcision ?
Won’ t those who are physically uncircumcised, but fulfill the law , judge you , who with the letter and circumcision are a transgressor of the law ?
For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly , neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh ;
but he is a Jew who is one inwardly , and circumcision is that of the heart , in the spirit , not in the letter ; whose praise is not from men , but from God .

nama: Némé mer yém Ju árét, o némé fénataye yém ghéngan néfégh yammén? 2 Tárfár yaman! Frondefifi, Ngánjánam ekafai a ewaramai yánjo si Ju árét. 3 Ndernáye wét ámb Ju ár kés tamorwén ndimbal mwighafnar? Némé yénayan so Ngánján ndimbal mwighafnar kamndangai? 4 Yaufifi! Ngánján siyém efalo, a méinyotio ár em unyégh ár. Ndené kés yéfarotaménd, "Ngánján, fém efalo so niyátote mbarkándaye njam noraya a so nawainde njam moghan ném."
english: Then what advantage does the Jew have ? Or what is the profit of circumcision ?
Much in every way ! Because first of all , they were entrusted with the revelations of God .
For what if some were without faith ? Will their lack of faith nullify the faithfulness of God ?

May it never be ! Yes , let God be found true , but every man a liar . As it is written , “ that you might be justified in your words , and might prevail when you come into judgment .” + 3:4 Psalms 51:4

nama: Ámb ár korayawét ndené, 'Mámáfi yam árké yéndfem ewafrotam yuyafotat fanfnenro ndené Ngánján siyém túmén mbarkánd. Yénémamén, Ngánján siyém yau mbarkánd yém sái tékratan yénékmeta yénd? 6 Yéné yau yém efalo! Ngánján túmén mwighé yéfarone némé yém mbarkánd a féyotaro. Yéné téfnan yémo so moghan ekmete méinyotio ár mband maratan.
english: But if our unrighteousness commends the righteousness of God , what will we say ? Is God unrighteous who inflicts wrath ? I speak like men do .
May it never be ! For then how will God judge the world ?

nama: Ámb ár mété norayat, "Yénd njam nuindan, yéné yam yufarote nderé ndimbal yém a mer Ngánján. Tane unyégh yamam yuyafote méinyo safén Ngánján túmén noraye némé yém efalo. Yénémamén, Ngánján yau so noraye ndené yénd wém yaufi ár. Yémo yau so kwékmangé tékratan." 8 Wénde yéné njam yém efalo, fém so wéi noraye, 'Yéndfem mé so ewafrotam mindefifi mámáfi yam. Féyo ámb mer yam so nufrote yéné téfnan.' Ámb ár wéi néngorayat yéné yém si yéndon árké ewatametan. Wénde njam fá korayangi ndené, yémofem yénd wasalitat. Yéné yém mbarkánd Ngánjánam so tékratan tákmangé yéné ár. Méinyotio ár séném yaufiyam ár
english: For if the truth of God through my lie abounded to his glory , why am I also still judged as a sinner?
Why not ( as we are slanderously reported , and as some affirm that we say ), “ Let ’s do evil , that good may come ?” Those who say so are justly condemned .

nama: Némé yénd so norayan yénamé? Yénd merro yéném? Yaufifi! Yénd té efalo nauyafongém ndené Ju ár a Téndo ár ndenaro em méinyotio yaufi mbanmbanan. 10 Ndené fété kés yéfarotaménd: Ámb efe yau mbarkánd yém, ámbiro ámb yau;
english: What then ? Are we better than they ? No , in no way . For we previously warned both Jews and Greeks that they are all under sin.
As it is written , “ There is no one righteous ; no , not one .

nama: efe ámb yau yém emo mat yiyátoté yau ámb emo yéronje Ngánján.
english: There is no one who understands . There is no one who seeks after God .

nama: Méinyotio té kangotat fá té nénafghangi nénáyátongi fifiofnar, efe yau yém ámbiro emo ewafrote mer, yau ámbiro ámb yém.
english: They have all turned away . They have together become unprofitable. There is no one who does good , no , not so much as one .” + 3:12 Psalms 14:1-3; 53:1-3; Ecclesiastes 7:20

nama: Yéfene kémngaféne sem ásauréghkaf mbéwé. Yéfene mbírmbíram yafrote unyégh. Yéfene si sem ndenanit kamban rarégh.
english: “ Their throat is an open tomb. With their tongues they have used deceit .” + 3:13 Psalms 5:9 “ The poison of vipers is under their lips .” + 3:13 Psalms 140:3

nama: Yéfene sénféne siyém waghanégh a njérmat si afoghare.
english: “ Their mouth is full of cursing and bitterness .”

nama: Yéfene kafkaf wékeye em ár ngiot.
english: “ Their feet are swift to shed blood.

nama: Awirorghafé a usérmboghafé eyota,
english: Destruction and misery are in their ways .

nama: krufrát ghérsé fá mafina em.
english: The way of peace , they haven’t known .”

nama: Kété yau yém Ngánján férfér yéfene ghékfan."
english: “ There is no fear of God before their eyes .”

nama: Féyo, yénd mat yéném ndené Ngánjánene nák yéfene yém Ju árfene. Ngánjánam emndai fá ménát méinyotio waitoghét árkéméné yáne nákam ewatamete. Yéné fifiyan, ámb ár so korayangé: 'Ngánjánam yau so tékratan kwékmangé yénd.' Méinyotio ár mband maratan so ewakai Ngánjánene sáfrére a yémo so emote fá. 20 Ngánjánene nákam ewauyafote árét safénro ndené yémofem té tawafrotat yaufi yam. Efe ámb yau so tiyátongé mbarkánd Ngánjánrére nák waitoghan. Yesum yéngwanai téfene tékrát
english: Now we know that whatever things the law says , it speaks to those who are under the law , that every mouth may be closed , and all the world may be brought under the judgment of God .
Because by the works of the law , no flesh will be justified in his sight ; for through the law comes the knowledge of sin.

nama: Wénde sénonjo yau ménaye nák waitoghe, mbarkánd yam kétamé Ngánjáneta mat té yényátoi. Wénde Mosesene nák a Ngánjánene mat yátogh árfene si té ténmaufrotawét yénémamén. 22 Yéné mbarkánd yam kétamé Ngánjáneta yéném ndimbal mwighamé Yesu Keriso kénjún yéfefaf méinyotio emofem efalo yiyátotat. Yau em fénataye kétamé Ju ár a Téndo ár, 23 ménamén yénd méinyotio té nényawayém yaufiyam kénjún a nénamndayém ténjfi Ngánjánene ndimbal kwayaghata, 24 a yényátoi mbarkánd yánjo ngáráme yénamé wanjarégh árké yénmormén Keriso Yesuefafmé. 25 Ngánjánam yuyafotam ndené Yesu Keriso kér yémormén wafatarégh wénan ghéngén téfene yaufiyammén. Ngánjánam yénmawite téfene táf njam efalo yiyátotam Yesu a yáne ghéngén férék. Ngánjánam yéné yafrotam ménát yuyafotam safén ndené fá siyém túmén mbarkánd a féyotaro. Tándái, Ngánjánam yau tékratan takmetau ár emofem mámáfi yam tawafrotawét. 26 Yémo yau takmetau tékratan fá ménamén fá témorwén métar. Fá mat témorwén némé yémo so tafrotau sénonjo, yéné efghon. Yémo yuyafoi ndené fá siyém féyotio mbarkánd a féyotaro. Yémo eumyote ár mbarkánd sái em yémafé táf njam efalo yiyátotat Yesu.
english: But now apart from the law , a righteousness of God has been revealed , being testified by the law and the prophets ;
even the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ to all and on all those who believe . For there is no distinction ,
for all have sinned, and fall short of the glory of God ;
being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus ,
whom God sent to be an atoning sacrifice through faith in his blood, for a demonstration of his righteousness through the passing over of prior sins, in God ’s forbearance;
to demonstrate his righteousness at this present time , that he might himself be just and the justifier of him who has faith in Jesus .

nama: Yénémaménnde, féyotaro yénd so nawarsam? Yau. Némé téfnan yénémamén? Téfene uwangogh yém meryam wafroghe? Yau, wénde ménaye yém ndimbal mwighaye. 28 Ár káyátongé mbarkánd Ngánjánafé yénamé ndimbal mwighayan, yau yénamé sái ewaitota nák. 29 Ngánján yau yém Ju árfene Ngánján naro. Fá wéi yém Téndo árfene Ngánján. Néméwét, fá siyém méinyotio árfene Ngánján. 30 Yéna yém ámbiroro Ngánján. Yémo so taumyongé Ju ár mbarkánd sái em yémafé táf njam efalo yiyátotat Keriso. Yémo wéi so eumyote ámb ár mbarkánd sái em yémafé táf yémofem njam efalo yiyátotat Keriso. 31 Némé yéndfem so ewironam nák yéné ndimbal mwighayan? Yaufifi! Yéndfem njam efalo yiyátotam Yesu, yéndfem taikrataye eyatotam Ngánjánene nák.
english: Where then is the boasting ? It is excluded . By what kind of law ? Of works ? No , but by a law of faith .
We maintain therefore that a man is justified by faith apart from the works of the law .
Or is God the God of Jews only ? Isn’ t he the God of Gentiles also ? Yes , of Gentiles also ,
since indeed there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith .
Do we then nullify the law through faith ? May it never be ! No , we establish the law .

nama: Némé so yénémamén norayam táf téfene afé Ebraam? Némnati némé yémo té yifoi. 2 Táf Ebraam njam té yiyátoi mbarkánd yam mer wafroghan Ngánjánam, fá so kawarsau yénémamén. Wénde yéné yau yémormén Ngánjánene ende. 3 Ndernáye wét Buk si noraye? "Ebraamém efalo tiyátotau Ngánján, a yéné téfnan fá yiyátoi mbarkánd yam."
english: What then will we say that Abraham, our forefather , has found according to the flesh ?
For if Abraham was justified by works , he has something to boast about , but not toward God .
For what does the Scripture say ? “ Abraham believed God , and it was accounted to him for righteousness .”

nama: Táf ár njam násáfotat, yéfenjo fai yau yém tambén, wénde tawanangi ménamén ásáfogh fá té tafrotat. 5 Wénde árké árém yau so ewafrotat ámb ásáfogh árkéménayam so eyátote mbarkánd Ngánjánafé. Wénde, yémofem njam efalo yiyátotat Ngánján, féyo Ngánjánam so taumyongé fá. Ménamén yéfene ndimbal mwighamén, Ngánján noraye yéné ár sem mbarkánd em yémafé. Yéné fiyém Ngánjánene tambén yémafayo emofem yiyáretat fá. 6 Deifid ndenéyameyo noraye yénémaményo njam korayau ároene frengéghmén endefaf Ngánjánam téramangé mbarkánd yam ndemamén yau téu ewafrotat ásáfogh yéné frengégh résaghét:
english: Now to him who works , the reward is not counted as grace , but as something owed.
But to him who doesn’ t work , but believes in him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is accounted for righteousness .
Even as David also pronounces blessing on the man to whom God counts righteousness apart from works :

nama: "Frengéghkaf fá em endefene yaufiyam tawalindangé, endefene yaufiyam rautoghkaf em.
english: “ Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven, whose sins are covered .

nama: Frengéghkaf yéné ár yém endene yaufiyam Yuram so yau ewawafte yéfefaf!"
english: Blessed is the man whom the Lord will by no means charge with sin.”

nama: Ngánjánam yéné yafrotam méinyotio árét, yéfe Ju ár a Téndo ár. Méinyotio ár efalo áuwéghan so em yénéyan, njam Ngánjánam taumyongé fá. Yénd té kénorayam ndené Ebraam ndimbal mwighayan Ngánjánam yiyátoi mbarkánd. 10 Yémofem ghéngan yénfayénd Ebraamene ménát yufarotayénd ndené fá témorwén Ju ár. Nemnati yéné efoghmén njam Ngánjánam yumyoi Ebraam. Yéné té nufrotam frondeyo yémofem ghéngan yénfayénd yáne, o yéné soramé? Yénd mat yéném ndené yéné yémormén frondeyo soramé ghéngan yanfayénd. 11 Ngánjánam yémndai Ebraam ndené ámb árém so ghéngan ténfangai. Yéné so témorwén mbor yáne fifiyan ménát so tufarotau ndené Ngánjánam té yumyoi. Ngánjánam té yumyoi Ebraam ménamén Ebraamém efalo yiyátoi fá. Ngánjánam yau yumyoi Ebraam ménamén yémofem ghéngan yanfayénd ndené Ju ár témorwén. Yéné yam yufarote ndené Ebraam siyém afé méinyotio árét árké Ngánjánam eumyoi. Ndené Ebraam, Ngánjánam eumyoi fá ménamén yémofem efalo tiyátotawét fá. Yéné yau yém ndimbal yam árém sái tanfetawét yéfene ghéngan. 12 Wénde Ebraam wéi siyém ndené afé méinyotio Ju árét emofem efalo yiyátotat Ngánján. Ámb árém té ghéngan tanfetawét yéné Ju ár, wénde fá kunfénmetawét Ebraamene ufaroghfaf. Yémofem efalo tiyátotawét Ngánján, ndené fété Ebraamém efalo tiyátotau frondeyo soramé yáne ghéngan yénfayénd.
english: Is this blessing then pronounced only on the circumcised , or on the uncircumcised also ? For we say that faith was accounted to Abraham for righteousness .
How then was it counted ? When he was in circumcision , or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision , but in uncircumcision.

He received the sign of circumcision , a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had while he was in uncircumcision, that he might be the father of all those who believe , though they might be in uncircumcision, that righteousness might also be accounted to them .
He is the father of circumcision to those who not only are of the circumcision , but who also walk in the steps of that faith of our father Abraham, which he had in uncircumcision.

nama: Ngánján unyéghofnar sie norayai Ebraamefaf a yáne ákiambumat, ndené mband marat so kamndangé yéfeyot ámb efghon. Ngánjánam yau unyéghofnar sie norayai ménamén Ebraamém tawaitotau nák. Ngánjánam unyéghofnar sie norayai ménamén Ebraamém efalo yiyátoi fá. Yéné téfnan Ngánjánam yumyoi Ebraam ndené fété mbarkánd ár yémafé. 14 Árém yau so tawanangi némat Ngánjánam unyéghofnar sie emndai ménamén yémofem ewaitotat nák. Njam yémofem tanangi yénaye, féyo ndimbal mwighé Ngánjánefaf so yém fifiofnar. A némé Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si yém fifiofnar so yém. 15 Mosesene nákam noraye ndené Ngánjánam so tékratan ekmete ár emofem yau ewaitotat nák. Wénde nákofnar, kété ámb yau yém árét sái ewaitotat.
english: For the promise to Abraham and to his offspring that he would be heir of the world wasn’ t through the law , but through the righteousness of faith .
For if those who are of the law are heirs , faith is made void , and the promise is made of no effect .
For the law produces wrath ; for where there is no law , neither is there disobedience.

nama: Yénémamén, Ngánjánam yénmaramai unyéghofnar si ndené tambén, ménamén fá siyém kénjtafnar. Yéndfem tawanangém táf njam yéndfem yafarotam fá. Njam yéndfem efalo yiyátotam Ngánján ndené Ebraam kés témorwén, féyo Ngánjánam ténmumyongé yénd. Yéné fiyém Ngánjánene unyéghofnar si méinyotio Ebraamene mérnat. Njam yéndfem efalo yiyátotam Ngánján, yéndfem so yémndetam téfene áki. Yéndfem so méinyotio ersam némé Ngánján unyéghofnar si té norayai. Yéné yau yém mé yéfendro emofem ewaitotat Mosesene nák. 17 Ndenáye fété Buk si noraye, "Yéndon té niyáton yéferafé méinyotio mband maratamé." Yéné fiyém némé Ngánjánamyo unyéghofnar si norayai, ménamén Ebraamém efalo yiyátoi fá. Ngánján yéné féyém emo eméinde ár nanotat kérta. Fá noraye yéné yammén kété árké yau mé em táf yéné njam té tamorwén kété.
english: For this cause it is of faith , that it may be according to grace , to the end that the promise may be sure to all the offspring, not to that only which is of the law , but to that also which is of the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all .

As it is written , “ I have made you a father of many nations .” This is in the presence of him whom he believed : God , who gives life to the dead , and calls the things that are not , as though they were .

nama: Njam Ngánjánam unyéghofnar sie noyarai Ebraam ndené fá so kénamndangé yéferafé mband maratoene, Ebraamém efalo tiyátotau fá. Ngánján wéi té norayai, "Fene ákiambum so em ndené wafoghofnar kénd." Yáne mé yém méghmégh njam yau yáne yém mer téfén méghméghot. 19 Ebraam té nénamndai ndené 100 yunjéf. Yáne fifi nénamdai bulu. Yáne amafausé, Sara wéi ndenáyo féyotarofnar témorwén ambum unjghét. Ebraam mat méinyotio témorwén yénémamén, wénde fá yau nékmboi Ngánján wafarogh. 20 Fá yau kékmbongai efalo yátoghét Ngánjánene unyéghofnar sian. Wénde yémo efalo tiyátotau Ngánján minde muyaye. Yémo efalo tiyátotau Ngánjánene ndimbal wafté. 21 Ebraam efalo yiyátoi ndené Ngánjánam so tawafrotau némat fá unyéghofnar si yémndai wafroghét. 22 Yénémamén yéndfem yéfandam ndené yéné siyém efalo: Ménamén Ebraamene ndimbal mwighamén, Ngánjánam yumyoi fá ndené fété mbarkándro yémafé.
english: Against hope , Abraham in hope believed , to the end that he might become a father of many nations , according to that which had been spoken , “ So will your offspring be .”
Without being weakened in faith , he didn’ t consider his own body , already having been worn out , ( he being about a hundred years old ), and the deadness of Sarah ’s womb .
Yet , looking to the promise of God , he didn’ t waver through unbelief, but grew strong through faith , giving glory to God ,
and being fully assured that what he had promised , he was also able to perform .
Therefore it also was “ credited to him for righteousness .”

nama: Nemnati yéné simén, 'Ngánjánam yumyoi fá ndené fété fá mbarkándro yém. Fá yau yáneménro norayat Ebraam, 24 wénde wéi téfeyot. Njam yéndfem efalo yiyátotam Ngánján, yémo so ténmumyongé yénd ndené fété mbarkándro yémafé. Ngánjánam yanoi kérta téfene Yuré Yesu, ménát fá nénamndai ghérsat wéikor yáne kér soramé. 25 Ngánjánam eumyoi ár yésmetaménd Yesu wafatarégh wénan téfene yaufiyammén. Féyo Ngánjánam yanoi Yesu kérta, ménát yuyafoi ndené yémo so yénd ténmumyongé ndené fété mbarkándro yémafé.
english: Now it was not written that it was accounted to him for his sake alone ,
but for our sake also , to whom it will be accounted , who believe in him who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead ,
who was delivered up for our trespasses , and was raised for our justification .

nama: Yénémaménnde, yénd efandam ndené Ngánján té yénmumyoi yénd ndené fété mbarkándro ménamén yéndfem efalo yiyátotam fá. Yéné téfnan, téfefaf yém krufrát Ngánjánafé ménamén némé téfene Yuré Yesu Kerisom té yafrotam téfeyot. 2 Téfene ndimbal mwighaye, Kerisom té yéné tuk sifayat yénérsam ánde yénd sénonjo yénmakai. A yénd féyotaroe méghméghafé néngretam kété sái yéném Ngánjánene ndimbal kwayaghayan. 3 Yénd áuwéghafé yéném mé njam yénd tékratan yéném. Yénd mat yéném ndené tékratam téfunangé awamarogh, 4 awamarogham yanote imángéghyam a imángéghyamam enyátote téfene méghmégh awanjarghét. 5 Yéné méghmégham yau so tinyátongé sotot yénd. Yénd mat yéném nderé uwégh yénd yénmuwetam, ménamén Ngánjánam té yénmaramai Ngánján Mbérmbér ménát sái tawafotau téfene tikéf yáne uwéghe.
english: Being therefore justified by faith , we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ ;
through whom we also have our access by faith into this grace in which we stand . We rejoice in hope of the glory of God .
Not only this , but we also rejoice in our sufferings , knowing that suffering produces perseverance ;
and perseverance , proven character ; and proven character , hope ;
and hope doesn’ t disappoint us , because God ’s love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who was given to us .

nama: Yénd njam mé ténmorwén muyafnar wáutáréghét yéndro, Keriso yénmormén mé yéné efghonfifi a kér yémormén téfe yaufiyam árét. 7 Tárfár ár yau so em sái kér em mbarkánd ároe, ndemamén ámb ár mété táf ménde yém sái kér yém árot efe yém minde mer. 8 Wénde Ngánjánam yuyafotam yánjo ndimbal uwégh téfefaf ménaye yénmáfáretam Keriso ménát kér sái témorwén téfeyot táf yaufiyaman mé sténmorwén. 9 Ngánjánam yénmumyoi ndené fété mbarkándro yémafé yánjo férke. Yénd féyotaroe mat yéném yémon so ténmanjarngé Ngánjánene nékwta. 10 Yémon té yényátoi fútar Ngánjánafé yánjo ambumoene kér kénjúmé táf yénd mé njam ténmorwén yáne mék téfnár. Sénonjo yénd Ngánjánene fútar yéném, yánjo Ambumom féyotaroe so ténmanjarngé yánjo ghérsaye. 11 Wénde yéné yau yém méinyotio! Yénd minde náuwetam sénonjo ménamén némé Ngánjánam té yafrotam téfeyot. Téfene Yuré Yesu Kerisom té yafrotam yéné féyotaroe. Yéné yém ménamén yánemén ndené yénd té sénonjo nénáyátongém Ngánjánene fútar. Kér Edémene kénjún, ghérsé Kerisoene kénjún
english: For while we were yet weak, at the right time Christ died for the ungodly.
For one will hardly die for a righteous man . Yet perhaps for a good person someone would even dare to die.
But God commends his own love toward us , in that while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us .
Much more then , being now justified by his blood, we will be saved from God ’s wrath through him .
For if while we were enemies , we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son , much more , being reconciled , we will be saved by his life .
Not only so , but we also rejoice in God through our Lord Jesus Christ , through whom we have now received the reconciliation .

nama: Yaufiyam nényawai mband maratot ámbiro ár kénjúmé, a yáne yaufiyamam yénérsam kér. Yéné ende, kéram méinyotio ár yanai, ménamén méinyotio árém yaufiyam nufrotam. 13 Soramé Ngánjánam nák eramai fronde yaufiyam témorwén mband maratan. Wénde yéné efghon, Ngánján yau ndené norayai ár kértat so tákmangai, ménamén kété yau tamorwén nák yéfeyot waitoghét. 14 Wénde mé kéram talimndau méinyotio ár, kétamé Edémene efoghta kétán Mosesene efogh. Méinyotio ár kér emormén, fá wéi efe yau nuyayénd ndené endeyo Edém snuyai.
english: Therefore , as sin entered into the world through one man , and death through sin, so death passed to all men because all sinned.
For until the law , sin was in the world ; but sin is not charged when there is no law .
Nevertheless death reigned from Adam until Moses , even over those whose sins weren’ t like Adam’s disobedience, who is a foreshadowing of him who was to come .

nama: Wénde kété yém ndimbalfifi fénataye yéfene néndkéfan Edémene yaufiyam a Ngánjánene raméghmén ngárám. Yéné ámbiro ár, Edémene yaufiyamam yénérsam kér méinyotio árét. Wénde nderé mindefifi Ngánjánene ngárám a ramégh yémormén ámbiro ároene kénjúmé, Yesu Keriso emon enérsam fénaté ndimbalfifi frengégh méinyotio árét. 16 Wéikor Ngánjánene ramégh ndené yau yém Edémene yaufiyammén fai kés yém. Edémene yaufiyamam yélimndam waghanéghfaf, wénde téfeyot yéna yém yuwek ramégh árké yumyote Ngánjánam, ndemamén yénd kértafé yéném tárfár yaufiyammén. 17 Edémene yaufiyamam yénérsam kér, yéné ámbiro ár kénjúmé. Nderé mindefifi emofem so tumyongi Ngánjánene ngárám a yáne mbarkánd yam ramégh, ménamén yéfe méinyotio emofem tanangi so emor yénayan waingégh yaufiyam a kér yéné ámbiro ár kénjúmé, Yesu Keriso.
english: But the free gift isn’ t like the trespass . For if by the trespass of the one the many died , much more did the grace of God and the gift by the grace of the one man , Jesus Christ , abound to the many .
The gift is not as through one who sinned; for the judgment came by one to condemnation , but the free gift followed many trespasses to justification .

For if by the trespass of the one , death reigned through the one ; so much more will those who receive the abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness reign in life through the one , Jesus Christ .

nama: Féyo, Edémene yaufiyamam yénérsam kértafé méinyotio árfefaf, wénde Kerisoene ámbiro mbarkánd yam wafrogham yénérse mbarkánd fútaryam yémafé Ngánján a totér ghérsé yéfeyot méinyotio árét. 19 Ménamén ámbiro árom yékenai Ngánján, méinyotio ár nénamndayénd yaufiyam ár. Wénde ménamén ámbiro árom yiyárai Ngánján, méinyotio ár so mbarkándro táyátongé.
english: So then as through one trespass , all men were condemned; even so through one act of righteousness , all men were justified to life .
For as through the one man ’s disobedience many were made sinners, even so through the obedience of the one , many will be made righteous .

nama: Ngánjánam ewaramai nák ménát yaufiyam narmboi. Wénde ánde yaufiyam narmboi, Ngánjánene ngárám narmboi minde ndimbal. 21 Ndené fété yaufiyamam talimndai ár kéran, sénonjo Ngánjánene ngárámam yénlimnde ndenáye, yénmaramete mbarkánd okayogh Ngánjánafé a yénérse yénd tuot ghérsat Yesu Keriso kénjúmé téfene Yuré.
english: The law came in that the trespass might abound ; but where sin abounded , grace abounded more exceedingly,
that as sin reigned in death , even so grace might reign through righteousness to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord .

nama: Ámb ár mé té noraya, 'Yéndfem so mé engwafrotam yaufiyam ménát Ngánjánene ngárám mé so narmbote.' 2 Yaufifi! Yénd kér té yénmormén yaufiyamta, ndernáye wéi yaufiyaman mé so yéngém? 3 Fém so mat em ndené méinyotio yénd efe té yénmálitotaménd Keriso Yesuene kénjút yénd nufnyayém yáfaf yáne kérfaf. 4 Yénd té yényatotaménd yémafé walitogh kénjúmé yáne kérfaf ménát, ndenáye Keriso yéngwano kérta Yárefafene ndimbal muyé kénjúmé, yénd wéi so yénmor totér ghérsaye.
english: What shall we say then ? Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound ?
May it never be ! We who died to sin, how could we live in it any longer ?
Or don’ t you know that all of us who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death ?
We were buried therefore with him through baptism into death , that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father , so we also might walk in newness of life .

nama: Ménamén njam yénd yémafé ámbiro yéném mérne yáne kér kénjúmé, yénd wéi ndenéyameyo so kérta nanotam ndené fá kés kérta nanoi. 6 Yénd mat yéném ndené téfene tándáwár fifioene ménde té enamndaméng wafatarégh wénan Yesufé. Yéné nufrotam ménát fifioene méndeyamam yau so yénlimnde yaufiyamot, a ménát yénd ndenayu yau so yéném yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár. 7 Ménamén árké ár njam kér yém yaufiyamta té nékmbo.
english: For if we have become united with him in the likeness of his death , we will also be part of his resurrection;
knowing this , that our old man was crucified with him , that the body of sin might be done away with , so that we would no longer be in bondage to sin.
For he who has died has been freed from sin.

nama: Féyo, yénd njam té kér yémormén Kerisofé, yénd nemnam ndené yénd wéi ghérsé so yéném yémafé. 9 Yénd mat yéném ndené ménamén Yesu kérta kés yéngwano, fá wéi yau so kér yém. Kér waftafnar yéngém yáfaf. 10 Njam Keriso kér yémormén, fá kér yémormén ámbirotaro ménát yironam yaufiyamoene wafté ambino. Sénonjo ndené fá yém ghérsé wéikor, fá yémor kété néuséne Ngánjáneyot.
english: But if we died with Christ , we believe that we will also live with him ,
knowing that Christ , being raised from the dead , dies no more . Death no longer has dominion over him !
For the death that he died , he died to sin one time ; but the life that he lives , he lives to God .

nama: Ndené endeyo, fémyo nafandati ndené kér té emormén yaufiyam wafroghta wénde ghérsé em Ngánjáneyot Keriso Yesuene kénjún. 12 Yénémaménnde, yau yumyotati yaufiyam sái elimnda féfene orngégh fifi ménát fémofem sái ewaitotati yaufioene ménde. 13 Yau eumyotati ámb fifi kafe yaufiyam wafroghét, ndené ásáfogh rokarnit mámáfiot, wénde karamangi fémyo Ngánjánefaf, ndené fété efe té kérta nénangoi ghérsat. A eumyotati féfenjo fifi kafe Ngánjánene mbarkánd yam wafroghét. 14 Yaufiyam yau so yém féfene Yuré, ménamén fém yau em nák mbanmbanan, wénde Ngánjánene ngárám mbanmbanan em. Mbarkánd yam faiofnar ásáfogh árfet
english: Thus consider yourselves also to be dead to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus our Lord .
Therefore don’ t let sin reign in your mortal body , that you should obey it in its lusts .
Also , do not present your members to sin as instruments of unrighteousness, but present yourselves to God as alive from the dead , and your members as instruments of righteousness to God .
For sin will not have dominion over you , for you are not under law , but under grace .

nama: Némé yénd so norayam ndené? Yéndfem yaufiyam mé so engwafrotam ménamén yénd yau nák mbanmbanan yéném wénde Ngánjánene ngárám mbanmbanan? Yau ámb fifi! 16 Fém efaloe mat té em ndené fém kénáyátongi faiofnar ásáfogh ár táf fémofem njam tiyátongi áyárghét. Fém so kénamndangi yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, árkéménayam so elimndat krot, o fémofem téu tékafangi áyárégh Ngánjáne, árkéménayam elimnde mbarkánd yamfaf. 17 Wénde kánjo Ngánjáne, ndemamén fém tamorwén yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, fémofem tikfan tékmetawét yéné watamegh árkéméné yéndfem ewaramayém. 18 Fém yuwek em yaufiyamta a té nénamndayénd mbarkánd yamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár.
english: What then ? Shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace ? May it never be !
Don’t you know that when you present yourselves as servants and obey someone , you are the servants of whomever you obey , whether of sin to death , or of obedience to righteousness ?
But thanks be to God that , whereas you were bondservants of sin, you became obedient from the heart to that form of teaching to which you were delivered .
Being made free from sin, you became bondservants of righteousness .

nama: Yéndon yéné mbanmban yafrotamén méinyotio efoghta ménamén yéné yém táikér matot. Fronde fémofem té taumyotawét féfenjo fifi kafe ménát tamorwén yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, fém yaufiyamotro tamorangérwén. Wénde sénonjo fém táf karamangi mbarkándoene faiofnar ásáfogh árot ménát wafroghét yéné árké yém njúnnjúnofnar.
english: I speak in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh ; for as you presented your members as servants to uncleanness and to wickedness upon wickedness, even so now present your members as servants to righteousness for sanctification.

nama: Fém njam mé tamorwén yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár, fém mé yau so kémnawét ménamén némé mbarkánd témorwén. 21 Némé mbwe yanayénd yéné efghon yéné rokarta? Yaufifi. Fém sénonjo wáiwái sem yénémamén. Yéné yam nufrotat kérfaf! 22 Wénde sénonjo fém yuwek em yaufita wénde té nénamndayénd Ngánjánene faiofnar ásáfogh ár. Yéné yam yénérse féfeyot ghérsé árké yém Ngánjánetro, a yéné yam ewaramete féfe tuot ghérsé. 23 Ménamén yaufiyamoene fai siyém kér, wénde Ngánjánene raméghmén siyém tuot ghérsé yáne kénjún Keriso Yesu téfene Yuré.
english: For when you were servants of sin, you were free from righteousness .
What fruit then did you have at that time in the things of which you are now ashamed ? For the end of those things is death .
But now , being made free from sin and having become servants of God , you have your fruit of sanctification and the result of eternal life .
For the wages of sin is death , but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord .

nama: Tane Fútaréf, fém mat té em yénd néngorayan árfefaf efe mat em nák, ndené nák waftafé ároefaf yém njamandio fá ghérsé yém? 2 Ufogh si, náke awerégh amaf mé yém yánjarefaf táf yánjar njam mé ghérsé yém, wénde njam yánjar kér yém, fá siyém awerégh nákta kefongé. 3 Njam wéi amafanyo ámb árfé ekmare njam yánjar ghérsé mé yém, amafom amafanyo nák eráfne. Wénde njam yánjar kér yém, fá té nafitrar yéné nákta a fá yau yém amafanyo nák rafargh ár njam ngawerangé ámb ár.
english: Or don’ t you know , brothers ( for I speak to men who know the law ), that the law has dominion over a man for as long as he lives ?
For the woman that has a husband is bound by law to the husband while he lives , but if the husband dies, she is discharged from the law of the husband .

So then if , while the husband lives , she is joined to another man , she would be called an adulteress . But if the husband dies, she is free from the law , so that she is no adulteress , though she is joined to another man .

nama: Tane fútaréf, fém wéi kér té emormén nákfaf Kerisoene fifi kénjúmé, ménát fém so em ámb ároene, yáne efe té yéngwano kérta, ménát yéndfem so efunam mbwe Ngánjáne. 5 Táf yénd njam ténlimndawét yaufiyam méndeyam, nák karmbotau yaufiyamoene ménde árké témorwén ásáfoghan téfene fifiyan. Kerkeran yaufiyamam yénlimnde kérfaf. 6 Wénde sénonjo, kér kétamé árkéménayam ténmumbetau yénd, yénd té yénferyéng nákta ménát yéndfem so yausáfotam Ngánjáne totér ende Ngánján Mbérmbéran, a yau tándáwár ende faroghmén náke. Nák a yaufiyam
english: Therefore , my brothers, you also were made dead to the law through the body of Christ , that you would be joined to another , to him who was raised from the dead , that we might produce fruit to God .
For when we were in the flesh , the sinful passions which were through the law worked in our members to bring out fruit to death .
But now we have been discharged from the law , having died to that in which we were held ; so that we serve in newness of the spirit , and not in oldness of the letter .

nama: Féyo, mé té ámb ár noraya, 'Nák yém yaufi. Yau! Yéné nákofnar, yénd yau mat so kwémorwén némé yaufiyam yém. Ámb nák noraye, 'Yau méndefé yéfanda ámb ároene rokar." Yéné nákonfar, yénd yau so mat kwémorwén ndené yéné yém mámáfi méndefé fandaghét ároene rokar. 8 Wénde yéné nákam yaramai yaufiyamoe féneféne ménát sái kényawangai tane mwighafaf. Yéné téfnan, yéndo yafngoyén ménde méinyotio yaufifaf. Njam kété yau yém nák sái yénmaufrotat némé yém mámáfi, féyo yaufiyam siyém waftafnar.
english: What shall we say then ? Is the law sin? May it never be ! However , I wouldn’ t have known sin except through the law . For I wouldn’ t have known coveting unless the law had said , “ You shall not covet .”
But sin, finding occasion through the commandment , produced in me all kinds of coveting . For apart from the law , sin is dead .

nama: Tándái yénd ghérsé mé kwémorwén frondeyo soramé yénd mat náyátoyén nák, wénde soramé nák nénufaryénd, yaufiyam sénjonjo muyafé témorwén tane ghérsayan, 10 a yénd kér wémorwén. Ngánjánam ewaramai yéné nák árét ménát mer ende ámnjoghét, wénde yéné nákam yénérsam kér tayot. 11 Yéné nákam yaramai yaufiyamoe féneféne ménát sái wasotam yénd. Féyo, yénd kér wémormén yéné nák téfnan. 12 Yénémamén, nák a yáne wafarégh em njúnnjúnofnar, a mbarkánd a merro.
english: I was alive apart from the law once , but when the commandment came , sin revived and I died .
The commandment which was for life , this I found to be for death ;
for sin, finding occasion through the commandment , deceived me , and through it killed me .
Therefore the law indeed is holy, and the commandment holy, righteous , and good .

nama: Féyo, ámb ár mé té noraya, 'Yéné fifi ndené yém ámb meram yénérsam kér féfefaf.' Yaufifi! Yaufiyamam yénérsam kér tayot. Yaufiyam Ngánjánene mer nákan nauyafotam ndené yénd kwémorwén kértafé. Yéné téfnan, yéndfem yéfandam ndené yaufiyam fété yém minde mámáfi. Ngánjánene mer nák kénjúmé yaufiyam násáfotam minde yaufiot.
english: Did then that which is good become death to me ? May it never be ! But sin, that it might be shown to be sin, was producing death in me through that which is good ; that through the commandment sin might become exceedingly sinful.

nama: Yénd mat yéném nák siyém mbérmbérafé em; wénde yénd wém njangén ár. Yénd ndenanit wém yaufiyamoene faiofnar ásáfogh ár. 15 Yéndon yau kor yérsan némé yéndon ewaufrotan. Némat yénd yéta ménde wém wafroghét yéndon yau yafrotan, wénde némé yéndon méndméndat yéfandan yéndon yafrotan. 16 A yéndon njam yafrotan némé méndefnar efandan, yéndon yiyátotan ndené nák siyém mer yém. 17 Féyo yéndon njam ewafrotan, yéné yau yéndonyo ewaufrotan, wénde yéné siyém yaufiyam yényau tafaf.
english: For we know that the law is spiritual , but I am fleshly, sold under sin.
For I don’t understand what I am doing . For I don’t practice what I desire to do ; but what I hate , that I do .
But if what I don’ t desire , that I do , I consent to the law that it is good .
So now it is no more I that do it , but sin which dwells in me .

nama: Yénd mat wém ndené yéna yau ámb mer yém tayot. Yénd wém njangén ár. Yénd yéta ménde wém wafroghét némé yém mer. Wénde yénd féyotarofnar wém wafroghét. 19 Yéndo yau ewafrotan mer yam árkéméné yénd yéta ménde wém, wénde yéndon mé ewafrotan yaufiyam árkéméné yénd méndefnar wém wafroghét. 20 Yéndon njam ewafrotan némé yénd yau ménde wém wafroghét, yéndon yau yéné ewafrotan, wénde yaufiyam yafrote tane kénjún.
english: For I know that in me , that is , in my flesh , dwells no good thing . For desire is present with me , but I don’ t find it doing that which is good .
For the good which I desire , I don’t do ; but the evil which I don’t desire , that I practice .
But if what I don’ t desire , that I do , it is no more I that do it , but sin which dwells in me .

nama: Féyo yénd mat té namndan némé nufrote tane kénjún. Njam yénd yéta ménde wém sái yafrotan mer yam, yéndon mé yénaro taferén yaufiro. 22 Méinyotio tane tikfe yéndon euwetan Ngánjánene nák. 23 Wénde yéndo yéfandan ámb nák ásáfoghan tane kénjún, yafrote mék nákfaf tane mwighayan a wiyátote yénd yaufiyam nákoene kwéfkwéf méngo ár árkéméné násáfote tane kénjún. 24 Némé afrengéghofnar ár yénd wém! Emo so kwanjarngé yéné fifita siyéngém kér? 25 Kánjo Ngánjáne, emo kwanjane tane kénjún Yesu Keriso téfene Yurane kénjúmé! Yénémaménnde, tane mwighaye yénd ménde wém sái ewaitotan Ngánjánene nák. Wénde táfandio, wénde yéndo yásáfotan yaufie ndené tane yuré, ménamén yénd wém njangén a ár.
english: I find then the law that , while I desire to do good , evil is present .
For I delight in God ’s law after the inward person,
but I see a different law in my members , warring against the law of my mind , and bringing me into captivity under the law of sin which is in my members .
What a wretched man I am ! Who will deliver me out of the body of this death ?
I thank God through Jesus Christ , our Lord ! So then with the mind , I myself serve God ’ s law , but with the flesh , sin’ s law .

nama: Yénémaménnde, yéfeyot ámb yau kértafé yém efe em Keriso Yesuene kénjún. 2 Ménamén ghérsé Ngánján Mbérmbéroene nákam Keriso Yesuene kénjúmé té yuwek tiyátoi fém kétamé yaufiyamoene nák a kroene nák. 3 Yéné nák árké Ngánjánam yaramai Mosese yau kor yényátoi yénd ndené kés. Yénd ténmorwén muyafnar ménát waitoghét yáne nák ménamén yénd ménd ár. Wénde Ngánjánam té yafrotam némé yáne nákam yau kor so tafrotau. Yémo yénmafaretam yánjo Ambum sái nénamndai ár ndené yénd siyéném. Yáne fifi témorwén ndené ár fifi emofem engwafrotat mámáfi. Ngánjánene Ambum kér yémormén ndené fété ghéngén ménát sái tawitau tékrat yam téfene yaufiyammén. Yéné ende, Ngánjánam yironam yaufioene wafté yéné árfene efe em fifie muyafnar. 4 Ngánjánam yéné yafrotam ménát nákoene ménde némat yém so nufrotat téfefaf, efe yau emor yaufioene ménde wafroghyaman wénde Ngánján Mbérmbér limánéghan.
english: There is therefore now no condemnation to those who are in Christ Jesus , who don’ t walk according to the flesh, but according to the Spirit .
For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus made me free from the law of sin and of death .
For what the law couldn’ t do , in that it was weak through the flesh , God did , sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for sin, he condemned sin in the flesh ,
that the ordinance of the law might be fulfilled in us who don’ t walk according to the flesh , but according to the Spirit .

nama: Fá efe elimnde yaufioene méndeyamam nemnat mé yaufi yamotro. Wénde fá efe emor Ngánján Mbérmbéran nemnat némé Ngánján Mbérmbér ménde yém. 6 Yaufioene ménde yamam yénlimndat kérot, wénde Ngánján Mbérmbéroene mwigham yénlimndat ghérsé a krufrát yamot. 7 Yaufioene ménde yamafé túmén násmét Ngánjánafé, ménamén yau ewaitote Ngánjánene nák, a yau féyotaro em waitoghét. 8 Fá efe elimndé yaufioene ménde yamam yau yéremngote Ngánján.
english: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh , but those who live according to the Spirit , the things of the Spirit .
For the mind of the flesh is death , but the mind of the Spirit is life and peace ;
because the mind of the flesh is hostile toward God , for it is not subject to God ’s law , neither indeed can it be .
Those who are in the flesh can ’ t please God .

nama: Wénde fém yau elimndé yaufioene ménde yam, fém elimndé Ngánján Mbérmbéram, táf njam Ngánjánene Mbérmbér yényau féfefaf. Endefefaf yau yényau Kerisoene Mbérmbér yéfene kénjún yau em Kerisoene. 10 Wénde Keriso njam yényau féfefaf, ndemamén féfene fifi kér so em yaufiyammén, wénde Ngánján Mbérmbéram ewaramete ghérsé féfeyot ménamén Kerisom té mbarkánd eyátoi Ngánjánafé. 11 A njam Ngánjánene Mbérmbér yém féfefaf emo yanoi Yesu kérta, fá emo yanoi Keriso kétamé kérta wéi so téramangé ghérsé kétán féfene ornégh fififaf yánjo Mbérmbére efe yényau féfefaf.
english: But you are not in the flesh but in the Spirit , if it is so that the Spirit of God dwells in you . But if any man doesn’t have the Spirit of Christ , he is not his .
If Christ is in you , the body is dead because of sin, but the spirit is alive because of righteousness .
But if the Spirit of him who raised up Jesus from the dead dwells in you , he who raised up Christ Jesus from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through his Spirit who dwells in you .

nama: Yénémamén, tane fútar, yénd yau so yénmor sái yénremngota fifioene ménde yaman. Yénd yau so ndenau yénmor ndenáye 13 Fém njam emor fifioene méndeye, fém so kér em. Wénde njam Ngánján Mbérmbére krot tákmangi féfene yaufiyam wafrogh, fém so ghérsé engém. 14 Ménamén fá efe elimnde Ngánján Mbérmbéram sem Ngánjánene ambum. 15 Fémo yau yéné mbérmbér yanai sái niyátota férférafé wénde fémo yanai ménát sái ném Yáne ambum a Yáne kénjún yénd nutnam, "Afé! Tane Afé!" 16 Ngánján Mbérmbéram wéi yémonyo efalo yiyátote téfene mbérmbérafé ndené yénd siéném Ngánjánene ambumtértér. 17 Ménamén yénd yéném Ngánjánene ambumtértér, yénd wéi mat yéném ndené yéndfem so tawinjongén mer rokar yá tambén. Yéné rokar em yémon árké té unyéghofnar si ewaramai yánjo ambumtértérat. Mérne yémafé Keriso, yéndfem so tawanangém rokar árké Ngánjánam té ewamnde yáyot. Yéné siyém efalo táf njam yéndfem eyátotam tékrat yam ndené Kerisom kés yafrotam. Féyo yéndfem wéi so yayamnetam ghérsé njafaran ndené Kerisom kés yafrote. Sénonjo tékrat a soramatau ndimbal kwayaghé
english: So then , brothers, we are debtors , not to the flesh , to live after the flesh .
For if you live after the flesh , you must die ; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body , you will live .
For as many as are led by the Spirit of God , these are children of God .
For you didn’ t receive the spirit of bondage again to fear , but you received the Spirit of adoption , by whom we cry , “Abba! Father !”
The Spirit himself testifies with our spirit that we are children of God ;
and if children , then heirs — heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ , if indeed we suffer with him , that we may also be glorified with him .

nama: Yéndon nemnan ndené sénonjomén tékrát yamafé yau so katmangé kétán soramé ndimbal kwayaghé sái nufrota téfefaf. 19 Méinyotio rokar Ngánján ewafrote nawanjete kétán ánde Ngánjánene ambumtértér so safén tákmangé. 20 Méinyotio rokar Ngánjánam ewafrotam nawengoyénd fifiofnarot, yau ménáyan yánjo méndeyan wénde ménamén Ngánjánene ménde témorwén. Wénde kété témorwén méghmégh, 21 yéné wafrogh rokar néretat kétán yéné efghot ánde so kafghangi yéfefaf Ngánjáne ambumtértérafefaf ndimbal kwayaghé kénjún yuwek kétamé kérta orngéghta.
english: For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which will be revealed toward us .
For the creation waits with eager expectation for the children of God to be revealed.
For the creation was subjected to vanity , not of its own will, but because of him who subjected it , in hope
that the creation itself also will be delivered from the bondage of decay into the liberty of the glory of the children of God .

nama: Yénd mat yéném ndené méinyotio wafrogh rokar tékratan yém ndené fété ambum áráfghan yénau tékrát yéfandat yénanotio sénonjo. 23 Yéné wéi yém ndenéyameyo téfe efe yéném efalo yátogh ár. Yéndfem té ewanyém Ngánján Mbérmbér ndené fété fronde yáne tambén téfeyot, wénde yénd wéi nendam yéndo kénjúm.Yéndfem té yanayém Ngánján Mbérmbér ndené fété fronde ramégh téfeyot, wénde wéi yénd ye nendam téfenjo kénjún. Yénd nawanjetam yéné efghot ánde Ngánjánam so tifongé yánjo ndimbal ásáfogh. Féyo yénd so yéném yáne féyotio ndené fété yáne ambumtértér. Téfene fifi so em yuwek kétamé yaufiyamoene waftata. 24 Yéné méghmégh kénjún yénd té yénmanjayéng. Wénde méghmégh árké efandam yéné méghmégh siyém fifiofnar yém. Efe méghmégh em yéné rokarotio táf té ewam? 25 Wénde yénd njam méghmégh yéném yéné rokarfaf yéndfem árkéméné yau efandam, yénd nawanjetam yénat métare.
english: For we know that the whole creation groans and travails in pain together until now .
Not only so , but ourselves also , who have the first fruits of the Spirit , even we ourselves groan within ourselves , waiting for adoption , the redemption of our body .
For we were saved in hope , but hope that is seen is not hope . For who hopes for that which he sees?
But if we hope for that which we don’t see, we wait for it with patience .

nama: Yéné endeyo, Ngánján Mbérmbéram wéi yénmáutáne téfene muyafnar sifayan. Ménamén yénd mafina yéném ndernáye so Ngánjánafé norayam. Wénde Ngánján Mbérmbér fáyo Ngánjánafé norayet téfeyot. Fá nutne Ngánjánefaf téfene téfnan yéné ende ár ánde yau kor so noraye. 27 Ngánjánam efande némé em téfene tikfan, a fá mat yém némé mwighé yém Ngánján Mbérmbéroene, ménamén Ngánján Mbérmbéram noraye yánjo ár téfnan Ngánjánene méndeyamé.
english: In the same way , the Spirit also helps our weaknesses, for we don’ t know how to pray as we ought . But the Spirit himself makes intercession for us with groanings which can ’ t be uttered.
